fact: April 15 is the 105th day of the year in non-leap years - that's exactly 15 weeks into the calendar!
In the Northern Hemisphere, dandelions are typically blooming everywhere around this time, turning lawns into bright yellow carpets.
Around April 15 in northern climates, night temperatures often still drop while days warm up quickly, creating crisp mornings.
Earthworms are usually very active underground around this time because the soil is finally warm and moist enough for peak wiggling.
The sun is climbing higher in the sky each day around April 15, making shadows shorter and daylight stronger.
In Australia and the Southern Hemisphere, leaves are typically turning brilliant autumn colors around April 15 as fall begins.
April 15 usually falls during Aries season in the zodiac, which runs from about March 21 to April 19.
In North America, maple trees are often producing peak sap flow around this time, perfect for syrup making.
Around April 15, days in the Northern Hemisphere are getting longer by about 2-3 minutes each day.
Bees are typically very busy around April 15 because spring flowers are blooming and providing lots of nectar to collect.
In many parts of North America, mid-April is prime tornado season because warm and cold air masses often collide.
April 15 is roughly one month after the spring equinox, marking the heart of the Northern Hemisphere's growing season.
